question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user flood GET /health/readiness to exhaust all PostgreSQL connection pool slots (configured via rosettaConfig.Db pool settings), causing the staking reward calculation pipeline to fail to acquire DB connections and permanently skip pending reward updates for the current staking period, resulting in theft of unpaid staking rewards for all staked accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: NewHealthController()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call GET /health/readiness to trigger the postgres.New(postgres.Config{DSN: rosettaConfig.Db.GetDsn()}) check, which opens a new DB connection on every invocation, exhausting the pool and blocking the EntityStakeCalculatorImpl from acquiring a transaction to call updateEntityStake(), causing pending_reward values to never be written and staking rewards to be permanently lost?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user send a crafted response to the internal /network/list call (by racing a port-reuse or local proxy scenario) to return an empty NetworkIdentifiers slice, causing networkList.NetworkIdentifiers[0] to panic with an index-out-of-range, crashing the Rosetta process and preventing staking reward transactions from being processed during the active staking period?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user time repeated GET /health/readiness requests to observe response latency differences in the rosettaClient.NetworkAPI.NetworkStatus() call, inferring when the staking period boundary is being processed, and use that timing oracle to front-run or interfere with staking reward payout transactions before pending_reward is committed?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: NewHealthController()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any rate limiting on GET /health/readiness to send thousands of concurrent requests, each triggering a 10-second PostgreSQL connection check (Timeout: time.Second * 10), holding open DB connections long enough to starve the staking reward importer of connections during the critical end-of-staking-period window, causing pending rewards to be skipped?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated GET /health/readiness route to continuously probe DB availability and infer the exact moment the entity_stake table is being updated, then submit a crafted transaction via the Rosetta construction API timed to race the pending_reward write, causing an inconsistent state where rewards are credited to the wrong account?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that checkNetworkStatus() calls rosettaClient.NetworkAPI.NetworkStatus() with no authentication, and the response includes the current block identifier, to determine the precise staking period boundary and time a balance-draining transaction to reduce stake_total_start before the pending_reward snapshot is taken, stealing rewards from other stakers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: NewHealthController()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated GET /health/readiness requests to cause the hellofresh/health-go library's health check goroutines to accumulate, leading to goroutine exhaustion that prevents the Rosetta server from processing staking reward-related block transactions, causing those transactions to be dropped and rewards to be permanently lost?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 10-second Timeout on the network health check to hold the readiness handler busy, and by sending enough concurrent requests, cause the Go HTTP server to exhaust its worker goroutines, making the /network/status endpoint unavailable and preventing the staking reward account (0.0.800) balance from being queried during reward calculation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: NewHealthController()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SkipOnErr: false setting on the postgresql health check to cause the readiness endpoint to return HTTP 503 continuously by flooding the DB connection pool, which in turn causes orchestration systems (Kubernetes liveness/readiness probes) to restart the Rosetta pod during staking period processing, losing in-flight pending_reward calculations?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user observe the difference in response time between a successful and failed rosettaClient.NetworkAPI.NetworkList() call to determine whether the Rosetta node is currently processing a staking reward block, and use that information to time a transaction that changes their staked_node_id just before the snapshot, inflating their pending_reward at the expense of other stakers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated /health/liveness and /health/readiness routes to perform a sustained DoS that causes the Rosetta server to be marked as unhealthy by its load balancer, routing all traffic away from it during the staking period end window, preventing staking reward transfer transactions from being submitted and causing rewards to expire unclaimed?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: NewHealthController()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that postgres.New(postgres.Config{DSN: rosettaConfig.Db.GetDsn()}) is called at construction time and the DSN string (including password) is stored in the health check closure, and if the health check library logs the DSN on error, the attacker can trigger a DB error via connection flooding to cause the DSN to appear in logs, then use those credentials to directly manipulate the entity_stake table and steal pending rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a request timeout on the outer GET /health/readiness HTTP handler (only the inner health check has Timeout: time.Second * 10) to hold connections open indefinitely via slow-read attacks, exhausting the server's connection table and preventing legitimate staking reward transactions from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that networkList.NetworkIdentifiers[0] is accessed without a length check, and by causing the /network/list endpoint to return an empty list (via a concurrent request that corrupts the in-memory network state), trigger a panic that crashes the health check goroutine and causes the readiness check to hang, blocking DB connections needed for staking reward processing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: NewHealthController()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 10-second PostgreSQL health check timeout to perform a time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) attack: the health check confirms DB is available, but the attacker floods the DB connection pool in the window between the health check passing and the staking reward calculation acquiring its connection, causing the calculation to fail silently and skip the pending_reward update?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the rosettaClient in checkNetworkStatus() is shared across all concurrent health check invocations without synchronization, and by flooding /health/readiness, cause a data race on the HTTP client's internal state, leading to corrupted network responses that cause the health check to incorrectly report the service as ready when the DB is in an inconsistent staking reward state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated /health/readiness endpoint to perform binary search on response times, determining whether the PostgreSQL DB is currently locked by the staking reward calculation transaction (entityStakeRepository.lockFromConcurrentUpdates()), and use that lock-detection oracle to time a competing transaction that reads stale pending_reward values before they are updated?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that checkNetworkStatus() makes an HTTP call to http://localhost:{port}/network/status, and if the attacker can influence the port value (e.g., through a misconfigured reverse proxy or port forwarding), redirect the health check to a malicious server that returns a crafted NetworkStatus response, causing the health check to always pass even when the real staking reward processing node is down?
